summary: Automation boolean value matching bug fix VERIFIED. The _values_match() function correctly handles comparison between MongoDB boolean values (True/False) and automation string values ('true'/'false'). The 'move to neck' automation now fires correctly when screens checkbox is set to True.

feature_tested.name: Automation Boolean Value Matching Fix
feature_tested.bug_description: Automation 'when screens changes to true, move to NECK' was NOT working because MongoDB stores checkbox as boolean True/False but automation watch_value is string 'true'. Strict comparison True != 'true' was failing.
feature_tested.fix_applied: New _values_match() function converts both values to lowercase strings before comparing: str(actual).lower() == str(expected).lower()

tests: test_02_automation_exists - PASS (verified 'move to neck' automation config)
test_06_screens_false_does_not_trigger - PASS (automation does NOT fire for screens=False)

manual_curl_test.description: Manual curl test to verify exact bug scenario
steps: 1. Created order with screens=false in SCHEDULING board
3. Response contained _automations_executed: [{name: 'move to neck', action: 'move_board', params: {target_board: 'NECK'}}]

critical_code_review_comments: FIX VERIFIED: _values_match() function correctly handles bool vs string comparison
str(True).lower() == 'true' enables MongoDB boolean to match automation string 'true'
Case-insensitive comparison works for both 'true'/'TRUE'/'True' and other string values
Automation fires on status_change trigger when watch_field is in changed_fields
String comparisons for other fields (blank_status, etc.) continue to work correctly
seed_data_creation: Created admin test session via MongoDB for API testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Automation boolean fix verified working. The _values_match() function in /app/backend/routers/automations.py (lines 90-102) correctly handles comparison between MongoDB boolean values and automation string values. The 'move to neck' automation (watch_field: screens, watch_value: 'true') fires correctly when order.screens is set to True (boolean). Test session: test_session_iter29_1772745952422

bug_rca.root_cause: MongoDB stores checkbox values as boolean (True/False) but automation watch_value is stored as string ('true'). The original check_conditions() function did strict comparison: order.get(watch_field) != watch_value which meant True != 'true' => False
reproduction_steps: 2. Create automation with watch_field='screens', watch_value='true', action=move_board to NECK
4. Before fix: Automation did NOT fire (True != 'true')
bug_rca.mitigation: Added _values_match() function that converts both values to lowercase strings before comparing. This handles: (1) bool to string comparison, (2) case-insensitive string comparison
